briefing:
# Atlantic Fintech Daily Briefing
### Friday, March 20, 2026

---

## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Banking-as-a-Service middleware provider Synapse, raising urgent questions about fintech deposit protection.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling growing Big Tech interest in leveraging open finance data for credit and payments.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what it means for incumbents and challengers alike.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving a new wave of niche, highly customized credit card products that challenge traditional card issuer models.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Bank of Canada holds key interest rate at 2.25%, saying war will boost global inflation](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bank-of-canada-interest-rate-9.7132984?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Bank of Canada held its benchmark rate steady at 2.25%, warning that the ongoing Iran war is expected to drive inflation higher globally and complicate the monetary policy outlook.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's government signalled a clear shift toward a formal open banking framework, calling screen scraping insecure and committing to a regulated API-based system — directly relevant as Canada's open banking rollout continues to evolve.

- **[1Password launches new platform to rein in companies' AI agents](https://betakit.com/1password-launches-new-platform-to-rein-in-companies-ai-agents/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian cybersecurity firm 1Password has launched a new platform designed to manage and secure AI agents operating within enterprise environments, addressing a growing fintech and enterprise security concern.

- **[Canada's population shrank last year — a first for the country, StatsCan says](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/canada-population-decline-first-time-9.7133643?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Statistics Canada reports that Canada's population declined for the first time on record, a demographic shift that carries significant implications for financial services demand, credit markets, and fintech growth strategies.

- **[How Ontario's innovation hubs scale startups across the province](https://betakit.com/how-ontarios-innovation-hubs-scale-startups-across-the-province/)** — *BetaKit* — A look at how Ontario's network of innovation hubs is helping startups scale, offering a potential model for Atlantic Canadian ecosystem builders seeking to grow regional fintech ventures.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Diesel prices surge to highest since 2022, raising concern for shipping, consumer costs](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/calgary/bakx-diesel-iran-kalibrate-9.7132148?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Diesel prices have hit their highest levels since 2022 amid the Iran conflict, a direct concern for Atlantic Canada's shipping-dependent economy and the embedded finance and lending products serving those industries.

- **[Colombia's budding tech scene needs a cash boost](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c4g2kyg4e78o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — Colombia's experience attracting venture capital to a growing but underfunded tech ecosystem mirrors challenges faced by Atlantic Canada's fintech sector, where access to early-stage investment remains a persistent barrier.

---

*📬 Curated by Atlantic Fintech | Building the fintech ecosystem across Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, PEI, and Newfoundland & Labrador.*
